name: Microrreserva Estrecho del Hocino — Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular desde Salobre
region_intro: Touring cycling around Salobre offers diverse landscapes, from fertile plains and valleys to mountainous regions and coastal views. The area features varied topography, including dramatic volcanic terrains and rolling hills, providing both scenic and challenging routes. Cyclists can explore charming inland white villages and routes that ascend towards higher elevations. Best touring cycling routes around Salobre The most popular touring cycling route is Microrreserva Estrecho del Hocino — Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular desde Salobre, a 22.3 miles (35.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses varied terrain, offering a balanced cycling experience. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Quejigal through the Sierra de Alcaraz — circular from Riópar Viejo, a difficult 22.8 miles (36.7 km) path. This route leads through the Sierra de Alcaraz, featuring significant elevation changes and scenic mountain views. Local touring cyclists also love the Closed road through a beautiful canyon – End of the greenway loop from Baños de la Esperanza, a 36.4 miles (58.5 km) trail leading through a beautiful canyon, often completed in about 4 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling around Salobre is defined by fertile plains, valleys, and mountainous regions. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Salobre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Salobre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Salobre?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Salobre, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Salobre?
A: Salobre offers incredibly diverse terrain for touring cyclists. You can expect to traverse fertile plains, ride through valleys abundant with tropical fruits, explore charming coastal towns with stunning Atlantic Ocean views, and ascend to inland white villages and mountainous regions. The area also features dramatic volcanic landscapes and rolling hills, providing both scenic and challenging routes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Salobre?
A: Yes, Salobre features several excellent circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063148159/microrreserva-estrecho-del-hocino-via-verde-de-la-sierra-de-alcaraz-circular-desde-salobre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Microrreserva Estrecho del Hocino — Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular desde Salobre&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 35.8 km trail. Another option is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063919764/quejigal-through-the-sierra-de-alcaraz-circular-from-riopar-viejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quejigal through the Sierra de Alcaraz — circular from Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 36.7 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the Salobre area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Salobre offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Closed road through a beautiful canyon – End of the greenway loop from Baños de la Esperanza&lt;/a&gt; is a 58.5 km trail with substantial elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor – Alcaraz loop from Salobre&lt;/a&gt;, a 63.8 km route with over 1300 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural landmarks or scenic viewpoints can I discover along the cycling routes?
A: The Salobre region is rich in natural beauty. You can find stunning natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Mundo River&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Peak&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5974287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Chorros Cave&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3484172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Landscapes Around Riópar&lt;/a&gt;, providing breathtaking views of the diverse topography.

Q: Are there any historical sites or towns to explore near the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass through or near picturesque towns and historical sites. You can explore the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4080509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt; and its significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5919080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor and Towers&lt;/a&gt;. The charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; is also accessible, offering glimpses into local life and history.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Salobre?
A: Salobre benefits from a generally favorable subtropical climate, making it suitable for touring cycling year-round. The mild temperatures contribute to enjoyable cycling experiences, though it's always wise to check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Salobre?
A: The touring cycling routes around Salobre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, which range from fertile plains to challenging mountain ascents, and the opportunity to explore charming villages and scenic natural features.

Q: Are there any facilities for cyclists, such as bike rentals or repair shops, in the Salobre area?
A: Yes, the Salobre area, particularly in resorts like Salobre Hotel Resort & Serenity, offers cycling-friendly infrastructure. This includes essential services such as bike rentals, repair workshops, secure storage, and washing areas, enhancing the touring experience. Some accommodations also provide cycling route suggestions.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with other outdoor activities in Salobre?
A: Absolutely! While exceptional for cycling, Salobre also offers a variety of other outdoor activities. You can enjoy hiking, golf on championship courses set amidst volcanic landscapes, horse riding through scenic mountain trails, and various water sports along the coast. This makes Salobre an ideal destination for a multifaceted outdoor adventure.

Q: Are there any less crowded touring cycling routes in Salobre?
A: While specific crowd levels can vary, exploring routes slightly off the main tourist paths or during off-peak hours can offer a more serene experience. Consider routes that venture deeper into the Sierra de Alcaraz, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bienservida – Plaza Mayor de Bienservida loop from Estación Villapalacios&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a challenging yet potentially quieter ride through mountainous terrain.
tips: Don Quixote Route that crosses the entire valley of the Estrecho del Hocino Microreserve, with views of the Salobre River canyon.

The Vía Verde de Alcaraz is divided into two sections: the first goes from Albacete to Alcaraz and the second from Reolid to the border with the province of Jaén. The road is not upgraded between Alcaraz and Reolid.

This point is the beginning of the second leg. There is a great picnic area with tables and some shade to rest before continuing the trip.
If there is ever a shortage of water on the Via Verde, you can organize supplies in Villapalacios.
The landscape changes in the "plateau". The olive plantations are gone, but now cattle farming and the addition of grain.

waypoints.name: Closed road through a beautiful canyon
Vía Verde of the Sierra de Alcaraz via Villapalacios
